e_visa.nationality: All nationalities not on visa-exempt list
e_visa.notes: Non-exempt nationals must apply for a Tourist Visa (120 days, 5,150 VUV fee) or Visitor Visa (30 days, 7,420 VUV fee) online before travel via the Vanuatu eVisa portal (evisa.gov.vu). Applications must be approved before travelling to Vanuatu. Employment, commercial, or business activities are not allowed on these visas. Extension is not allowed for Tourist Visa.

visa_policy.notes: Exempt nationals receive a Tourist Visa on arrival valid for up to 120 days, or a Visitor Visa on arrival valid for 30 days. Non-exempt nationals must apply and receive approval before travelling to Vanuatu via evisa.gov.vu. Visa-exempt nationals can enter as tourists, for family visits, or for business meetings/conferences, but may not engage in employment or commercial activities. The official exempted-countries page contains typographical errors and duplicate/contradictory entries (Bahrain appears in both lists; Monaco and Rwanda appear in both lists; 'Russian' and 'Federation' are two entries for Russia; 'Manibia' is Namibia; 'Phillipines' is Philippines; 'Swaziland' is now Eswatini). Turkey was previously noted as ambiguous (appeared in both exempted and non-exempted sections); multiple official and semi-official corroborating sources (Wikipedia Visa policy of Vanuatu citing immigration.gov.vu; Wego listing 40 exempt European countries including Turkey; embassies.net) confirm Turkey is in the Europe exempted group - Turkey is now classified visa_free as of 2026-06-27 re-validation. Note: The EU removed Vanuatu FROM the EU visa-exemption list in December 2024 (meaning Vanuatu passport holders now need a Schengen visa); this action does NOT affect EU citizens entering Vanuatu, who remain on Vanuatu's own visa-exempt list.

visa_types.name: Tourist Visa (eVisa)
visa_types.purpose: For non-exempt nationals wishing to visit Vanuatu for tourism, family visits, or short-stay purposes. Employment, commercial, or business activities are not permitted.
visa_types.notes: Available via evisa.gov.vu for nationalities not on the visa-exempt list. Extension is not allowed. Fee is 5,150 VUV. Must be approved before travelling to Vanuatu.

visa_types.name: Visitors Visa
visa_types.purpose: For visitors intending to stay in Vanuatu for up to 30 days. Requires proof of intent to depart and financial capacity.
visa_types.notes: Fee 3,600 VUV. Requires completed Visitor Application Form, valid passport, return ticket, proof of funds. Also available via evisa.gov.vu (fee 7,420 VUV on that portal). 30-day stay limit evidenced by requirement to depart within 30 days.

visa_types.name: Extended Visitors Visa
visa_types.purpose: Extension of a visitor's stay in Vanuatu for up to 4 months (from initial entry). For those who need to extend beyond their original visitor visa.
visa_types.notes: Fee 6,000 VUV for a visa valid up to 4 months. Applied for in-country. Requires Extended Visa Application Form, valid passport, photographs, return ticket, evidence of reasons and funds. Governed by Immigration Act 2010 (Sections 42, 43, 46, 60) and Immigration Visa Regulation Order 2011.

visa_types.name: Interim Visa
visa_types.purpose: A transitional visa for applicants awaiting a decision on a longer-stay visa or whose status is otherwise pending. Requires undertaking from a Vanuatu citizen.
visa_types.notes: Fee 10,000 VUV. Applied for in-country. Requires Extended Visa Application Form, passport copy, photographs, return ticket, evidence of reasons, funds, and undertaking from a Vanuatu citizen. Governed by Immigration Act 2010 (Sections 27(a), 31(a)).

visa_types.name: Transit Visa
visa_types.purpose: For travellers passing through Vanuatu in transit to a third destination.
visa_types.notes: Fee 1,800 VUV. Requires Transit Visa Application Form, valid passport, travel itinerary. Governed by Immigration Act 2010 (Sections 34–37, 75–76) and Immigration Visa Regulation Order 2011.

visa_types.name: Student Visa
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals enrolled in an educational institution in Vanuatu. Requires evidence of enrollment and medical examination.
visa_types.notes: Fee 2,880 VUV (for applicants under 18; fees for adults not stated on portal page). Requires Student Visa Application Form, valid passport, evidence of enrollment, security deposit, evidence of funds, medical examination. Governed by Immigration Act 2010 (Sections 35(1), 37(a-e)).

visa_types.name: Business Visa
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals visiting Vanuatu for business-related activities, including meetings, conferences, and employer-sponsored visits.
visa_types.notes: Fee 20,000 VUV for single entry. Requires Business Visa Application Form, valid passport, photographs, letter from employer, letter of acceptance from employer, employer's business registration or license, proof of relationship (if family), and government cashier receipt. Governed by Immigration Act 2010 (Section 44) and Immigration Visa Regulation Order 2011.

visa_types.name: Special Category Visa
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals who do not fall under standard visa categories; for specific circumstances as defined by the immigration authority.
visa_types.notes: Fee not specified on portal. Requires Special Category Visa Application Form, valid passport, photographs, certified birth certificate, police clearance, medical examination, marriage/death/divorce certificates as applicable, and letter of recommendation. Governed by Immigration Act 2010 (Sections 32, 44(a-e), 45(4)).

visa_types.name: Residence Visa
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals seeking to reside in Vanuatu for 1 year based on employment, land ownership, foreign investor status, or self-funding.
visa_types.notes: Fee 57,600 VUV for applicants aged 18+ for a 1-year visa. Requires Residence Application Form, valid passport, photographs, police clearance, medical examination, certified birth/marriage/death/divorce certificates, supporting documents based on status (employment letter, land ownership proof, foreign investor documentation, or self-funding evidence), and security deposit. Governed by Immigration Visa Regulation Order 2011 (Articles 6, 12, 16).

visa_types.name: Permanent Residence Visa
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals seeking permanent residency in Vanuatu. Grants long-term right to reside in Vanuatu.
visa_types.notes: Fee not specified on tradeportal page; evisa.gov.vu data indicates 206,000 VUV for individuals, 463,500 VUV for family of 4, 100,000 VUV per additional child. Requires Vanuatu Permanent Residence Application Form. Governed by Immigration Act 2010 and Immigration Visa Regulation Order No. 180 of 2011. Note: Cannot engage in employment, commercial, or business activities.

visa_types.name: Diplomatic Visa
visa_types.purpose: For non-citizens holding diplomatic passports on a diplomatic mission for a foreign government in Vanuatu, including their family members.
visa_types.notes: Fee-exempt. Duration at Director's discretion. Multiple entry. Only for holders of diplomatic passports on official diplomatic missions.

visa_types.name: Provisional Investor Visa
visa_types.purpose: For non-citizens intending to invest in Vanuatu who are in the phase of setting up a business while communicating with VFIPA and VFSC.
visa_types.notes: Fee 51,500 VUV. Duration 1 year, multiple entry. Extendable. Can change status to Business Investor Visa. Applicant must be in phase of setting up a business while communicating with VFIPA and VFSC.

visa_types.name: Business Investor Visa
visa_types.purpose: For individuals who have been granted a Foreign Investment Approval Certificate by VFIPA, a certificate of registration from VFSC, or a business license from relevant authorities.
visa_types.notes: Fee 206,000 VUV. Duration 3 years, multiple entry, renewable. Can change status to Direct Investor Visa or Permanent Investor Visa. Requires VFIPA Foreign Investment Approval Certificate, VFSC registration, or business license.

visa_types.name: Direct Investor Visa
visa_types.purpose: For individuals who have deposited a bond into a nominated Government bank account for 8 years and provided proof of sufficient funds to cover ongoing living expenses.
visa_types.notes: Fee 309,000 VUV. Duration 8 years, multiple entry. Can change to Investor Visa or Leasehold Owner Visa. Requires bond deposit into nominated Government bank account for 8-year term and proof of sufficient funds for living expenses.

visa_types.name: Permanent Investor Visa
visa_types.purpose: For foreign investors who have been residing lawfully and continuously in Vanuatu for at least 10 years and are maintaining ownership and management of their business.
visa_types.notes: Fee 3,090,000 VUV. Duration indefinite, multiple entry. Requires 10 years of lawful continuous residence in Vanuatu and active ownership/management of business.

visa_types.name: Retiree Visa
visa_types.purpose: For non-citizens aged 50 or above proposing to retire in Vanuatu. Cannot engage in employment, commercial, or business activities.
visa_types.notes: Fee 309,000 VUV. Duration 10 years, multiple entry. Extendable. Applicant must be aged 50 or above. Cannot engage in employment, commercial, or business activities.

visa_types.name: Self-Funded / Leasehold Holder Visa
visa_types.purpose: For non-citizens with leasehold ownership of property certified to be worth VT 10,000,000 or more, and monthly income of at least VT 250,000 per person. Must reside physically in Vanuatu.
visa_types.notes: Fee 59,330 VUV. Duration 12 months, multiple entry. Requires leasehold ownership of property certified at VT 10,000,000 or more, and income of at least VT 250,000/month per person (max 1,000,000 VUV). Must reside physically in Vanuatu. Can change to investor visa.

conditional_access.conditions: Diplomatic Visa is given to non-citizens who hold diplomatic passports and are on diplomatic mission for a foreign government in Vanuatu with their family members. Duration is at Director's discretion. Multiple entry. Fee-exempt.

cbi.program_name: Vanuatu Citizenship by Investment (Programs: DSP/VCP, CIIP/Form D, REO)

options.notes: Development Support Program (DSP) / Vanuatu Contribution Program (VCP): Non-refundable contribution to the Vanuatu National Development Fund. Government-mandated fees: Single applicant USD 130,000; Married couple USD 150,000; Married couple + 1 child USD 165,000; Married couple + 2 children USD 180,000; Additional dependent USD 10,000 each. FIU Diligence Fee USD 5,000. Application fee 5,000 VUV (paid upon lodging). Citizenship fee paid only after Commission approval. Source: vancitizenship.gov.vu/index.php/citizenship/fees-and-charges

Real Estate Option Program (REO): Investment in a Vanuatu Government Approved Project (VGA Project). Commenced July 23, 2021. Four currently approved projects: Pacific Springs (Efate), Narpow Point Coral Bay (Efate), FPF Rainbow City Project (Efate), Milai (Espiritu Santo). Citizenship fees per family size structure apply on top of property investment. Minimum property investment amount not stated verbatim on official page. Source: vancitizenship.gov.vu/index.php/news-and-updates/101-real-estate-option-program-reo

Capital Investment Immigration Plan (CIIP) / Form D: Application fee USD 250. Citizenship fee USD 260,000 covers applicant, spouse, and 1 child under 18. Additional child under 18: USD 19,250 per person. Dependent aged 18–21: USD 44,250 per person. FIU Diligence Fee USD 5,000. Source: vancitizenship.gov.vu/index.php/citizenship/fees-and-charges

cbi.processing_time: Not specified on official pages; 3 months allowed to complete oath and pay fees after Commission approval (with possible 2-month extension upon written request).
cbi.notes: The Vanuatu Citizenship Office (vancitizenship.gov.vu) operates under the Office of the Prime Minister. Programs confirmed active: DSP/VCP, CIIP (Form D), REO. Dual citizenship policy is not stated explicitly on any official page found. Residency is NOT required for CBI programs. The EU removed Vanuatu from its visa-exemption list in December 2024 partly due to concerns about the CBI program. CBI fees confirmed directly from official fees-and-charges page (June 2026). Applicants should confirm current fees directly with the Citizenship Office as fees may be updated.

programs.name: Business Investor Visa (formerly Foreign Investor Residence Visa)
programs.notes: For individuals who have been granted a Foreign Investment Approval Certificate by VFIPA, a certificate of registration from VFSC, or a business license from relevant authorities. Duration: 3 years, multiple entry, renewable. Fee: 206,000 VUV. Can change status to Direct Investor Visa or Permanent Investor Visa. Source: evisa.gov.vu/visas

programs.name: Direct Investor Visa
programs.notes: For individuals who have deposited a bond into a nominated Government bank account for a term of 8 years and provided proof of sufficient funds to cover ongoing living expenses. Duration: 8 years, multiple entry. Fee: 309,000 VUV. Can change to Investor Visa or Leasehold Owner Visa. Source: evisa.gov.vu/visas

programs.name: Permanent Investor Visa
programs.notes: For foreign investors who have been residing lawfully and continuously in Vanuatu for at least 10 years and are maintaining ownership and management of their business. Duration: Indefinite, multiple entry. Fee: 3,090,000 VUV. Source: evisa.gov.vu/visas

programs.name: Provisional Investor Visa
programs.notes: For non-citizens intending to invest in Vanuatu who are in the phase of setting up a business while communicating with VFIPA and VFSC. Duration: 1 year, multiple entry. Fee: 51,500 VUV. Extendable. Can change status to Business Investor Visa. Source: evisa.gov.vu/visas

programs.name: Self-Funded / Leasehold Holder Visa
programs.notes: Leasehold Holder Visa: For non-citizens with leasehold ownership of property certified to be worth VT 10,000,000 or more, and income of at least VT 250,000 per month per person (max 1,000,000 VUV). Duration: 12 months, multiple entry. Fee: 59,330 VUV. Must reside physically in Vanuatu. Can change to investor visa. Source: evisa.gov.vu/visas

programs.name: Retiree Visa
programs.notes: For non-citizens aged 50 or above proposing to retire in Vanuatu. Duration: 10 years, multiple entry. Fee: 309,000 VUV. Extendable. Cannot engage in employment, commercial, or business activities. Source: evisa.gov.vu/visas

programs.name: Permanent Residency Visa
programs.notes: Permanent residency visa granted for a period not exceeding 10 years. Fee: 206,000 VUV for individuals; 463,500 VUV for family of 4; 100,000 VUV per additional child. Extendable. Requires valid passport copy and police clearance. Cannot engage in employment, commercial, or business activities. Source: evisa.gov.vu/visas

agreements: PACER Plus (Pacific Agreement on Closer Economic Relations Plus - labour mobility provisions)

validation_notes: MAJOR: Added ~60 countries missing from the previous record: all of Americas/Caribbean (Argentina, Antigua & Barbuda, Bahamas, Barbados, Belize, Bermuda, Brazil, Canada, Chile, Cuba, Dominica, Grenada, Guyana, Jamaica, Mauritius, Mexico, Peru, St Kitts & Nevis, St Lucia, Trinidad & Tobago, Uruguay, St Vincent & Grenadines, USA); Western Europe (UK, Austria, Belgium, Denmark, Finland, France, Germany, Greece, Ireland, Italy, Liechtenstein, Luxembourg, Netherlands, Norway, Portugal, Romania, Spain, Sweden, Switzerland, Cyprus, Malta, Andorra, Vatican City/Holy See); Asia (Bangladesh, Brunei, China, India, Israel, Japan, Kuwait, Malaysia, Maldives, Bahrain); Africa (Namibia, Eswatini/Swaziland, Russia). All confirmed directly from immigration.gov.vu/visa-exempted-countries/ via Firecrawl stealth scrape.
CORRECTION: Namibia was missing; added as NAM (official page typo 'Manibia').
CORRECTION: Eswatini added as SWZ (official page uses obsolete name 'Swaziland').
CORRECTION: Russia added as RUS (official page lists as 'Russian' and 'Federation' - two spurious entries for one country).
CORRECTION: Previous record was flagged as 'only ~55 countries confirmed'; full list now has ~115 visa-free entries confirmed from the live official page.

CORRECTION: E-visa notes updated to clarify both Tourist Visa (120 days, 5,150 VUV) and Visitor Visa (30 days, 7,420 VUV) are available for non-exempt nationals via evisa.gov.vu. max_stay_days updated from null to 120.

CORRECTION: RBI section substantially expanded with fees confirmed from evisa.gov.vu: Business Investor Visa (206,000 VUV), Direct Investor Visa (309,000 VUV), Permanent Investor Visa (3,090,000 VUV), Provisional Investor Visa (51,500 VUV), Leasehold Holder Visa (59,330 VUV), Retiree Visa (309,000 VUV), Permanent Residency Visa (206,000 VUV individual).
CORRECTION: Diplomatic Visa added to conditional_access with basis=passport_type for diplomatic passport holders of any nationality.
MAINTAINED: CBI fees confirmed unchanged from official page: DSP single USD 130,000, Form D (CIIP) USD 260,000.

NOTE: Turkey appears in BOTH the exempted (Europe section) and non-exempted (Asia/Others) lists on the official page - this is an apparent official inconsistency. Turkey has been excluded from visa_free pending official clarification (noted in data_quality.gaps).
NOTE: Bahrain appears in both exempted and non-exempted lists - exempted section entry retained as primary; noted in the entry.
NOTE: Monaco appears in both exempted and non-exempted lists - exempted section entry retained as primary; noted in the entry.
NOTE: Rwanda appears in both exempted and non-exempted lists - exempted section entry retained as primary; noted in the entry.

2026-06-27 RE-VALIDATION: immigration.gov.vu returned HTTP 403 during this session; official source could not be directly re-fetched. Cross-referenced against Wikipedia 'Visa policy of Vanuatu' (which cites immigration.gov.vu), embassies.net aggregation, and web search snippets citing the official page. Five entries ADDED to visa_free: Iceland (ISL), Turkey (TUR), Cameroon (CMR), Mozambique (MOZ), Macau SAR (MAC) - all confirmed by at least two corroborating secondary sources citing immigration.gov.vu. TURKEY GAP RESOLVED: Multiple sources (Wikipedia, Wego, embassies.net) confirm Turkey is in the Europe exempted group of 40 countries; Turkey reclassified from 'gap' to visa_free. TOTAL visa_free entries now ~122 (including territories). No entries were removed from prior record - conservative approach applied (preservation beats guessing when direct official re-fetch is unavailable).

2026-06-29 RE-VALIDATION: immigration.gov.vu returned HTTP 403 again; direct official page re-fetch blocked. Cross-referenced against embassies.net (2026), Wikipedia 'Visa policy of Vanuatu', and Google web search snippet directly quoting the official page. FINDING: Iceland (ISL) was confirmed on the official page by (a) embassies.net 40-country Europe list including Iceland, and (b) Google search snippet of immigration.gov.vu. Iceland was previously noted in data_quality.gaps as unresolved; now ADDED to visa_free. FINDING: Albania, Armenia, Georgia, Kosovo, Montenegro confirmed NOT on visa-exempt list (they appear in the NON-EXEMPT section of the official page per Google snippet and are listed as requiring visas on embassies.net). GAP RESOLVED for these five countries. Seychelles (SYC) - present in current file but not in Wikipedia or embassies.net lists - RETAINED per preservation rule (was in original immigration.gov.vu crawl; requires direct official page access to confirm/deny). TOTAL visa_free entries now ~123 (including territories). No entries removed - conservative approach.
gaps: Direct official re-fetch of immigration.gov.vu/visa-exempted-countries/ was blocked (HTTP 403) during 2026-06-27 and 2026-06-29 re-validations. All additions and confirmations are based on secondary sources (Wikipedia Visa policy of Vanuatu, embassies.net) citing the official page and/or Google web search snippets quoting immigration.gov.vu - not a direct official page read. Verify directly when the site becomes accessible.

Albania, Armenia, Georgia, Kosovo, Montenegro: Confirmed NOT visa-exempt as of 2026-06-29 re-validation. These appear in the NON-EXEMPT section of the official page (per Google search snippet of immigration.gov.vu content) and are listed as requiring visas on embassies.net. They were never added to this file. Gap now closed.
Exact minimum bond deposit amount for the Direct Investor Visa is not stated verbatim on the evisa.gov.vu page (only the visa fee of 309,000 VUV is confirmed).
Minimum property investment amounts for the REO citizenship program are not stated verbatim on the official REO page.
Dual citizenship policy is not stated explicitly on any official page found.
Processing time for CBI programs is not stated verbatim on official pages.
Path to citizenship from residence visa programs (Permanent Investor Visa, etc.) is not stated on official pages.
Whether Vanuatu is formally part of any freedom-of-movement area (beyond PICTA/MSG trade provisions) could not be confirmed from official sources.

The income requirement for the Self-Funded/Direct Investor Visa category (formerly noted as VT 20,000,000 assets) could not be re-verified directly from the new evisa.gov.vu page which uses different criteria.

Conditional credential-based access rules (e.g., entry permitted if holding a valid US/Schengen visa) are not documented on any official Vanuatu source found. May exist in unpublished bilateral instruments.

Bermuda, New Caledonia, Niue, Cook Islands: Listed in prior official-source crawl of immigration.gov.vu but absent from Wikipedia/embassies.net cross-references. However, a Google search snippet quoting the official page does mention 'New Caledonia' and 'Niue'; and the official page is known to include some territories. Retained in file (preservation rule); status should be re-confirmed when direct official page access is restored. Vatican City (VAT): Confirmed as separate from Holy See entry on Wikipedia list; retained.

Seychelles (SYC): Present in this file from prior official crawl but NOT present in either Wikipedia Visa policy of Vanuatu or embassies.net 2026 aggregation. Retained per preservation rule; requires direct official page re-confirmation.

Macau SAR (MAC): Added in this session based on Wikipedia citing immigration.gov.vu. iso3 'MAC' is the standard ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 for Macau; verify against the exact wording used on the official Vanuatu page.

outbound_official.note: No official Vanuatu government outbound visa-list page located; rely on destination-side data. Vanuatu's Ministry of Foreign Affairs (mfaicet.gov.vu) publishes a 'Visa Waiver Agreements' page (https://mfaicet.gov.vu/index.php/consular-services/visa-waiver-agreements) and a Travel Advisory page (https://mfaicet.gov.vu/index.php/consular-services/travel-advisory) under its Consular Services section, both oriented toward outbound travel by Vanuatu nationals. However, neither page publishes a comprehensive machine-readable or enumerated list of visa-free / visa-on-arrival / e-visa / ETA destinations for Vanuatu ordinary-passport holders. The travel advisory page contains only specific conflict-zone warnings (e.g., Israel/Middle East as of March 2026). The visa-waiver-agreements page did not return enumerated country content via fetch. Vanuatu's Department of Immigration and Passport Services (immigration.gov.vu) publishes no outbound list either - its visa-exempted-countries page lists nationalities exempt from visas TO ENTER Vanuatu (inbound), not where Vanuatu citizens may travel. No analog to India's mea.gov.in/vffin page was found for Vanuatu.
